refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication packet loss at or below the customer-defined threshold (acceptance criterion: ≤1% under agreed network conditions) to ensure safe and effective remote equipment operation.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-009 addresses a critical quality-of-service requirement for teleoperation. Packet loss directly impacts command latency, feedback fidelity, and operator situational awareness—all safety-critical factors in remote heavy equipment control. Refinement clarifies that the requirement is conditional on 'agreed network conditions' and ties acceptance to a measurable, customer-negotiated target rather than a fixed value.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct packet loss measurement under representative network conditions (latency, jitter, bandwidth constraints) agreed with customer. Measure end-to-end packet loss across the teleoperation communication path (operator input → vehicle → operator feedback) using network monitoring tools. Validate against acceptance criterion in both nominal and degraded network scenarios.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-009.1: The teleoperation system shall detect and report packet loss metrics in real-time.
FR-NGHE-009.2: The teleoperation system shall implement error detection and recovery mechanisms to minimize impact of lost packets on command and feedback integrity.
FR-NGHE-009.3: The teleoperation system shall provide operator notification when packet loss exceeds acceptable thresholds.
FR-NGHE-009.4: The teleoperation network link shall be provisioned and configured to achieve the customer-defined packet loss target under agreed conditions.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant communication channels (dual network paths) with automatic failover to reduce single-point-of-failure packet loss impact.
DP-B: Deploy adaptive bitrate encoding and forward error correction (FEC) in the teleoperation protocol stack to recover lost packets without retransmission.
DP-C: Provision dedicated, quality-of-service (QoS) managed network link with traffic prioritization and congestion control to minimize packet loss at the network layer.
DP-D: Combine network-layer QoS provisioning (DP-C) with application-layer FEC and adaptive encoding (DP-B) to achieve defense-in-depth packet loss mitigation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement a defense-in-depth packet loss mitigation strategy combining (1) QoS-managed network link provisioning with traffic prioritization and congestion control, and (2) application-layer forward error correction and adaptive bitrate encoding in the teleoperation protocol stack.

potential_sources: Network link provisioning (DP-C component) couples to infrastructure and carrier agreements; must be coordinated with site network planning.
Application-layer FEC (DP-B component) couples to teleoperation codec and protocol design; must be integrated into command/feedback serialization.
Adaptive bitrate encoding couples to operator experience and vehicle sensor bandwidth; requires tuning for acceptable latency and fidelity trade-offs.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a composite solution addressing packet loss at multiple layers. Couplings are manageable through clear interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) and staged integration. No circular dependencies detected. Decoupling risk is low if network QoS and application FEC are treated as independent subsystems with well-defined handoff points.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Customer-agreed network conditions are stable and measurable (e.g., defined latency, jitter, available bandwidth).
Assumption: Teleoperation protocol stack can be modified to support FEC and adaptive encoding without breaking backward compatibility.
Risk: Network QoS provisioning may not be achievable in all deployment scenarios (e.g., remote worksites with limited carrier SLA options).
Risk: FEC overhead may increase latency or reduce effective bandwidth, requiring careful tuning to maintain operator responsiveness.
Risk: Adaptive bitrate encoding may degrade video/sensor feedback quality during congestion, affecting operator situational awareness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-D to explicitly separate concerns: (1) Network Link DP: 'Provision QoS-managed network link with traffic prioritization and congestion control.' (2) Teleoperation Protocol DP: 'Implement forward error correction and adaptive bitrate encoding in teleoperation codec.' This separation clarifies ownership boundaries and allows independent verification of each layer's contribution to packet loss mitigation.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: The Teleoperation module owns the end-to-end teleoperation communication system, including protocol stack, codec, FEC, adaptive encoding, packet loss detection, and operator notification. It interfaces with the Vehicle module (via Tasking and Telemetry) and Safety Services module (via SafetyAlert). It depends on the Network Link interface for underlying transport.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (movement, tool control, etc.) received from operator console; Teleoperation module decodes and forwards to Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sensor data (position, orientation, load, camera feed, etc.) encoded and transmitted to operator console; Teleoperation module applies FEC and adaptive encoding.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Packet loss threshold breach notifications sent to Safety Services for operator alerting and potential operation suspension.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Underlying network transport (QoS-managed, provisioned for target packet loss); Teleoperation module sends/receives encoded packets over this link.

module_containment_scores.notes: Teleoperation is the natural owner of packet loss mitigation logic (FEC, adaptive encoding, detection, reporting). All teleoperation-specific protocol and codec logic resides here. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link interface dependency (external) and SafetyAlert notification (cross-module, but localized).

Vehicle module owns vehicle control and sensor acquisition, not teleoperation communication quality. Assigning packet loss mitigation to Vehicle would scatter protocol logic across vehicle firmware and operator console, violating cohesion. Not recommended.

Safety Services owns safety policy enforcement and alerting, not communication protocol implementation. Packet loss detection and FEC are communication concerns, not safety policy. Safety Services should consume SafetyAlert notifications from Teleoperation, not own the mitigation logic.

NGHE Solution is the top-level system container. Assigning packet loss mitigation here would bypass the Teleoperation module's natural responsibility and create diffusion across multiple subsystems. Not recommended.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the optimal owner of DP-D (defense-in-depth packet loss mitigation). It encapsulates all teleoperation-specific protocol, codec, FEC, and adaptive encoding logic. It interfaces cleanly with Vehicle (via Tasking/Telemetry), Safety Services (via SafetyAlert), and Network Link (transport dependency). Containment score of 95 reflects high cohesion and minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Risk: Network Link provisioning (QoS, traffic prioritization) is owned by infrastructure/network team, not Teleoperation. Teleoperation must depend on Network Link interface contract; if QoS is not delivered, packet loss mitigation at application layer alone may be insufficient. Mitigation: Define clear Network Link SLA and acceptance criteria.

Risk: SafetyAlert notification to Safety Services creates a cross-module dependency. If Safety Services response is slow or unavailable, operator may not be alerted to packet loss threshold breach. Mitigation: Implement local operator notification in Teleoperation as primary alert; SafetyAlert as secondary escalation.

Risk: Adaptive bitrate encoding may conflict with operator expectations for consistent video/sensor quality. Mitigation: Make adaptive encoding tunable and provide operator visibility into quality degradation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Network Link interface includes QoS provisioning and traffic prioritization as part of its contract. If Network Link is not yet designed, escalate to ensure QoS is a design requirement, not an afterthought. Additionally, clarify the SafetyAlert interface: define packet loss threshold values, notification latency requirements, and expected Safety Services response actions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (defense-in-depth packet loss mitigation) directly addresses FR-NGHE-009 (teleoperation packet loss ≤1%). The combination of network-layer QoS and application-layer FEC/adaptive encoding provides multiple mechanisms to achieve the acceptance criterion. DP-D is technically sound and feasible. No circular logic or missing coverage detected.

diffusion_risks: Network Link provisioning is external to Teleoperation module; Teleoperation depends on Network Link SLA being met. If Network Link does not deliver QoS, Teleoperation's FEC/adaptive encoding alone may not achieve packet loss target. Mitigation: Define and enforce Network Link acceptance criteria.

SafetyAlert notification crosses module boundary to Safety Services. Ensure SafetyAlert interface is well-defined and Safety Services response is timely. Mitigation: Define SafetyAlert contract (threshold, latency, expected actions).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module contains all teleoperation-specific packet loss mitigation logic (FEC, adaptive encoding, detection, reporting). External dependencies (Network Link, SafetyAlert) are minimal and well-scoped. Containment score of 95 is justified. No significant diffusion detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-009 is feasible via network testing under agreed conditions. Packet loss can be measured end-to-end using network monitoring tools. Acceptance criterion (≤1%) is quantifiable and testable. Teleoperation module's packet loss detection and reporting mechanisms support verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of DP-D to Teleoperation module is clear. Teleoperation owns protocol, codec, FEC, and adaptive encoding. Network Link interface provides transport. SafetyAlert interface provides safety escalation. No ambiguity in responsibility or interface ownership.

recommended_revisions: Refine Network Link interface contract to explicitly include QoS provisioning, traffic prioritization, and target packet loss SLA. Ensure Network Link design is aligned with Teleoperation's packet loss mitigation strategy.
Define SafetyAlert interface specification: packet loss threshold values, notification latency requirements, expected Safety Services response actions, and escalation procedures.
Decompose FR-NGHE-009 into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-009.1 through 009.4) and assign each to appropriate module/layer (Teleoperation, Network Link, Safety Services) for detailed design and verification.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-009 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs (packet loss detection, error recovery, operator notification, network provisioning) that span multiple modules and design layers. Decomposition is necessary to allocate responsibility, define interfaces, and enable detailed design and verification. DP-D is sound and Teleoperation is the correct IM owner, but sub-level design is required to realize the full solution.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-009.1: The teleoperation system shall detect and report packet loss metrics in real-time (ownership: Teleoperation module).

FR-NGHE-009.2: The teleoperation system shall implement error detection and recovery mechanisms (FEC, adaptive encoding) to minimize impact of lost packets on command and feedback integrity (ownership: Teleoperation module).
FR-NGHE-009.3: The teleoperation system shall provide operator notification when packet loss exceeds acceptable thresholds (ownership: Teleoperation module with Safety Services escalation).

FR-NGHE-009.4: The teleoperation network link shall be provisioned and configured to achieve the customer-defined packet loss target under agreed conditions (ownership: Network Link / Infrastructure team).

assumptions: Customer-agreed network conditions are stable, measurable, and documented (latency, jitter, bandwidth, packet loss target).
Teleoperation protocol stack can be modified to support FEC and adaptive encoding without breaking backward compatibility or introducing unacceptable latency.
Network Link interface will be designed with QoS provisioning and traffic prioritization as core requirements.
Safety Services module will respond to SafetyAlert notifications within acceptable latency (TBD).
Operator console and vehicle firmware can be updated to support FEC and adaptive encoding.
open_risks: Network QoS provisioning may not be achievable in all deployment scenarios (remote worksites, limited carrier SLA options). Mitigation: Define fallback strategies and acceptance criteria for degraded network conditions.
FEC overhead may increase latency or reduce effective bandwidth, affecting operator responsiveness. Mitigation: Conduct latency and bandwidth trade-off analysis during detailed design.

Adaptive bitrate encoding may degrade video/sensor feedback quality during congestion, affecting operator situational awareness. Mitigation: Define quality degradation thresholds and operator notification requirements.

SafetyAlert notification latency may be insufficient to prevent unsafe operations during packet loss events. Mitigation: Define SafetyAlert SLA and implement local operator notification as primary alert mechanism.

Packet loss detection accuracy depends on telemetry feedback; if feedback is lost, detection may be delayed. Mitigation: Implement redundant detection mechanisms (e.g., command echo, timeout-based detection).
evidence_needed: Network conditions specification (latency, jitter, bandwidth, packet loss target) agreed with customer.
Teleoperation protocol specification including FEC, adaptive encoding, and packet loss detection mechanisms.
Network Link interface contract including QoS provisioning, traffic prioritization, and target packet loss SLA.
SafetyAlert interface specification including threshold values, notification latency, and expected Safety Services response.
Latency and bandwidth trade-off analysis for FEC and adaptive encoding.
Network testing results demonstrating packet loss ≤1% under agreed conditions.
Operator feedback on video/sensor quality degradation during adaptive encoding.
Safety Services response time measurement for SafetyAlert notifications.
